rationale: Contract-stability lens. Create(dir string, in CreateInput) error is a sane, idiomatic surface for Bead 4's cobra RunE. CreateInput exposes exactly the seven plain values Bead 4 resolves (BeadID *string, Spec, Target, Round, ExpectedReviewers, ApproveThresholdExpr, ReviewedHeadSHA) — nothing missing, no *config.Config/executor/git type leaked; create.go imports only stdlib and the config-free leaf-invariant check passes. Error semantics are correct for a RunE: Create returns plain fmt.Errorf-wrapped errors (internal/panel does not import guard), leaving the ADR-0035 guard.NewFailure + recovery line to Bead 4's adapter — the intended division. Critically, Create interpolates no untrusted flag value (target/bead) into its error text — only dir/briefPath (derived from the slug Bead 4 validates first) and fixed marker constants — so there is no newline-injection path from a Create error into a forged recovery: line (supports the 109-final-G2 concern). The BRIEF stub is cleanly consumable by Bead 5: the machine header is the single home of the 'Your job' verdict contract (verdict enum, top-level hard_block sibling, reviewer-authored fields read presentation-only), byte-consistent with the schema doc; the stub body carries exactly the four skill-authored sections with TODO(skill) placeholders. Re-panel regenerates the header while preserving the skill body byte-for-byte. Builds green, three named tests pass, tree clean.
findings: SIGNATURE (sane): Create(dir string, in CreateInput) error maps 1:1 onto a cobra RunE — Bead 4 resolves root/config/SHA/layout, then calls panel.Create(dir, panel.CreateInput{...}). Field set is complete for stamping expected_reviewers/approve_threshold/reviewed_head_sha/round/bead_id/spec/target. CreateInput was the plan-sanctioned alternative to Panel (Bead 1 step 1); the wire shape stays the existing Panel struct.

PLAIN-VALUES-ONLY (honored): CreateInput fields are only *string/string/int; no config/git/executor types. create.go imports encoding/json, fmt, os, path/filepath, strings only. `deps=$(go list -deps ./internal/panel) && ! grep internal/config` passes (verified).

ERROR SEMANTICS (usable for RunE, ADR-0035): Create returns plain wrapped errors, not guard.NewFailure — correct, since internal/panel must stay a leaf and the recovery-line ceremony belongs to Bead 4's CLI adapter. Marker errors name the open/close counts (actionable). No untrusted flag value (target/bead) reaches any Create error string, so Bead 4 can safely wrap err.Error() into a guard.NewFailure recovery body without a newline-forgery vector; the only interpolated value is dir/briefPath, derived from the slug Bead 4 validates before calling.

BRIEF-STUB (consumable by Bead 5): renderBriefHeader is the single verdict-contract home inside the delimited region, so Bead 5 step 1's removal of the skill's re-authored 'Your job' block leaves exactly one instruction. Header payload contract (verdict enum APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/REJECT, hard_block as top-level sibling never per-finding, five reviewer-authored fields read presentation-only) matches interfaces.md § Panel Artifact Schema and plan step 2/4. Stub body sections (Summary / Files in Scope / Prior-Round Asks / Lens) match ms-panel-run step-3 fill targets.

OBSERVATION (non-blocking, likely intended): a re-panel Create rebuilds Panel from CreateInput alone, so any prior abandoned/abandon_reason in panel.json is cleared (CreateInput has no such field). Abandonment is out of Bead 1's stated scope and clearing-on-new-round reads as correct un-abandon semantics; flagging only so Bead 4/5 are aware Create is a full-overwrite, not a field-preserving merge.

OBSERVATION (non-blocking, caller's responsibility): Create does not validate Round>=1; Round 0 would render a nonconforming `<slot>-round-0.json` instruction. Consistent with the leaf-takes-plain-values-on-faith contract (same posture as the ReviewedHeadSHA doc comment); Bead 4 defaults round to 1.

HEADS-UP for Bead 4 (not a Bead 1 defect): plan § Bead 4 step 1 prose still reads `panel.Create(dir, Panel{...})`; the shipped signature takes CreateInput, so Bead 4 must call panel.Create(dir, panel.CreateInput{...}). Bead 1 step 1 explicitly sanctioned the CreateInput choice.
